chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-6208 of 2023 M/s The Benitoz & others Versus Federation of Pakistan & others Date Order with signature of Judge For orders as to maintainability of petition. Dated: 16.01.2024 Mr. Darvesh Mandhan for petitioners. -.-.- Counsel claimed that he has not challenging the vires of SRO 966(I)/2022 dated 30.06.2022, however it is claimed that it has lived upto its effectiveness and not available for enforcement due to statutory limit. The case of petitioners is yet to be matured in the sense that not even Goods Declarations (GDs) have been filed. It is only claimed that no sooner the GDs are filed, the petitioners may be compelled to deposit such amount in the shape of regulatory duty in terms of above SRO, which has lived its life. Unless that cause is triggered the petition is premature. Even under the circumstances if the SRO is applied, as raised and questioned in the instant petition, it could be agitated before the concerned authority/forum under the hierarchy. In these circumstances, the petition, being misconceived is dismissed along with listed applications. Judge Judge
